findings.message: The chosen Option A no longer satisfies the inherited standalone outcome. This end-state keeps Caryina's Node/SOAP refund backend unchanged for Axerve, and the risk table later says Option A may remain permanent if REST access never arrives. That conflicts with the stated target at line 63 and the planned Phase 5 cleanup at line 151: Caryina payment refund infrastructure cannot actually be removed if it remains the long-term Axerve execution service.

The proposed `https://caryina-host/admin/api/refunds-internal` service path is blocked by Caryina's current auth gate. `apps/caryina/src/proxy.ts` redirects every `/admin/:path*` request except `/admin/login` and `/admin/api/auth` unless a valid admin cookie is present, so a token-only internal endpoint under `/admin/api/*` will not be reachable without an explicit matcher/exemption redesign.

The analysis still treats inventory-uploader auth as the correct template for a payment admin surface, but that session implementation fails open when KV revocation checks are unavailable (`apps/inventory-uploader/src/lib/auth/session.ts:82-113`). That may be acceptable for an internal inventory tool; it is not a free carry-over for payment management without an explicit hardening decision.

The webhook-log model is over-scoped from a false baseline. Cover-me-pretty already persists Stripe webhook processing through `handleStripeWebhook()` into the shared `StripeWebhookEvent` store (`packages/platform-core/src/stripeWebhookEventStore.ts`). Creating a second `PaymentWebhookEvent` pipeline via internal API after processing duplicates an existing contract instead of extending it.

"All 9 existing Caryina refund tests remain valid without modification" is too strong. Once Phase 2 turns `/admin/api/refunds` into a thin proxy and introduces a new internal Axerve route, the current public-route tests no longer match the route's behavior and will need to be split or updated.

The analysis still overstates portfolio-wide runtime provider switching. The only concrete Phase 3 integration is Caryina's `resolveCaryinaPaymentProvider()`. Cover-me-pretty has no equivalent runtime provider resolver today and still hardcodes "billingProvider": "stripe" in `apps/cover-me-pretty/shop.json`, so the "per-shop processor selection" goal remains under-scoped.
The analysis correctly carries forward the `StripeWebhookEvent` name collision and identifies renaming as a necessary prerequisite before any Prisma migration work.
